     The Numbers Business Report

The Revenant (2015)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $184,056,789Details
International Box Office $349,300,408
Worldwide Box Office $533,357,197
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $18,927,765
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $21,038,200
Total $39,965,965
Further financial details...

Synopsis

In an expedition of the uncharted American wilderness, legendary explorer Hugh Glass is brutally attacked by a bear and left for dead by members of his own hunting team. In a quest to survive, Glass endures unimaginable grief as well as the betrayal of his confidant John Fitzgerald. Guided by sheer will and the love of his family, Glass must navigate a vicious winter in a relentless pursuit to live and find redemption.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$474,560 (0.3% of total gross)
Legs:4.62 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:34.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$135,000,000 (worldwide box office is 4.0 times production budget)
Theater counts:4 opening theaters/3,711 max. theaters, 7.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $254,358,961

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 25th, 2015 (Limited) by 20th Century Fox
January 8th, 2016 (Expands Wide) by 20th Century Fox
March 13th, 2026 (IMAX) by 20th Century Studios (10th Anniversary)
International Releases: January 6th, 2016 (Wide) (Austria)
January 6th, 2016 (Wide) (Germany)
January 7th, 2016 (Wide) (Russia (CIS))
January 7th, 2016 (Wide) (Bahrain)
January 7th, 2016 (Wide) (United Arab Emirates)
Video Release: March 22nd, 2016 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for strong frontier combat and violence including gory images, a sexual assault, language and brief nudity.
(Rating bulletin 2392, 9/23/2015)
Running Time: 156 minutes
Award nomination: 2016 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Award win: BAFTA Best Film Winner, Directors Guild of America Outstanding Directorial Achievement in Theatrical Feature Film Winner, Golden Globe Winner Best Picture—Drama
Cast, crew, or production detail: Development Hell
Film format: IMAX: DMR
Plot point: Animals Gone Bad, Betrayal, Dream Sequence, Left for Dead, Native Americans, Revenge
Subgenre: Survival Adventure
Time period setting: 1800s
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: RatPac Entertainment, Anonymous Content, M. Productions, Appian Way, Regency Enterprises, New Regency
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Full financial estimates for this film, including domestic and international box office, video sales, video rentals, TV and ancillary revenue are available through our research services. For more information, please contact us at research@the-numbers.com.


  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

In an expedition of the uncharted American wilderness, legendary explorer Hugh Glass is brutally attacked by a bear and left for dead by members of his own hunting team. In a quest to survive, Glass endures unimaginable grief as well as the betrayal of his confidant John Fitzgerald. Guided by sheer will and the love of his family, Glass must navigate a vicious winter in a relentless pursuit to live and find redemption.

Opening Weekend:$474,560 (0.3% of total gross)

Domestic Share:34.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)

Theater counts:4 opening theaters/3,711 max. theaters, 7.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $254,358,961
